architecture_fit.note: Does not implement equality lookup: routes mass via a hardcoded +20/-20 bias keyed on oracle p1/p2 label positions, not on token matching.

baseline_comparison.note: Shows the task's uniform baseline in plots, but the comparison is meaningless since the method beats it by reading the answer, not via a real mechanism.

faithfulness.note: Actively unfaithful — the inert one-hot QK contributes nothing (Q[p2] zeroed); all match_mass comes from the label-leaking positional boost. No ablation.

operating_range.note: L=8..64 holds at ~1.0, but the perfect range is trivial because the oracle boost makes L irrelevant; under one order of magnitude swept.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-built, but the hand-set weights depend on oracle p1/p2 positions, so it is not a valid hand-set equality circuit.

visual_judgement.note: Bar/lift/heatmap/circuit plots are clean and readable, though the heatmap is reconstructed by hand and encodes the same shortcut.

visualisation_rationale.note: README.md is absent entirely — no 'Why this visualisation' section to justify chart choices.

notes: Despite near-perfect benchmark numbers (equality_robustness ~1.0), the mechanism is a label leak: it reads the planted pair's oracle positions p1/p2 from the batch and adds a +20/-20 bias to place attention directly on p1, while the one-hot QK 'equality' construction is inert (Q[p2] is zeroed). It therefore does not compute equality at all, fails faithfulness, and ships no README documenting the approach or visualisation rationale. The benchmark cannot detect this since it only measures attn[p2,p1].
